professional_summary: Computer Science student and aspiring Software Engineer with experience in teaching programming, co-founding community-focused foundations, and developing full-stack applications. Proven track record in designing modular system architectures, securing government funding, and delivering structured technical instruction. Skilled in React, Node.js, and .NET, with a strong focus on building scalable, user-centric solutions.

responsibilities: Collaborated with a Product Owner and senior engineering consultant to design a 15-phase multi-agent orchestration system.
Architected a server-side routing engine to dispatch 16 specialised agents based on pipeline state.
Engineered modular agents with defined responsibilities, JSON state contracts, and turn-based execution controls.
Reduced research-phase token usage by 60% by integrating Tavily API.
Implemented a parallel assembly phase with 5 concurrent agents.
Designed an evidence-based validation layer to prevent unsupported or fabricated claims.
achievements: Eliminated race conditions and reduced latency from LLM-based routing.
Improved system throughput and reliability via modular design and parallel execution.

responsibilities: Delivered structured programming instruction across multiple Melbourne schools (JavaScript, C, Java, Python).
Explained complex technical concepts to students (12–20 per session).
Provided hands-on debugging and problem-solving support.
Collaborated with educators and coordinators to deliver consistent learning experiences.
Created a promotional video for the company website.
achievements: Awarded High Distinction for outstanding placement performance.
Improved student comprehension and engagement across varying skill levels.

responsibilities: Planned and delivered community programs engaging 500+ youth across Victoria.
Led the Intergenerational Conversations Youth Conference.
Facilitated dialogue between youth and community elders on mental health.
Engaged in grant writing and stakeholder engagement.
achievements: Secured $50,000 in government funding (20% above target).
Achieved 4.5/5 satisfaction rating for youth conference.
